history.label: c. 870
history.text: According to Landnamabok, Swedish Viking Gardar Svavarsson wintered at Husavik around 870.

history.label: c. 870
history.text: Nattfari and two enslaved people were left behind when Gardar departed, creating an early farm settlement.

history.label: Name
history.text: The name Husavik means bay of houses, probably referring to Gardar's homestead at the site.
Husavikurkirkja, the town's well-known wooden church, was built and became a key local landmark.
Until 2004, Husavik served as an export harbour for silica extracted from the Lake Myvatn area.

history.label: Today
history.text: Modern Husavik is shaped by fishing, tourism, whale watching, GeoSea baths and its harbour on Skjalfandi bay.
